sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

Broadcom faced a downgrade due to software growth concerns, while Eli Lilly and AbbVie moved forward with clinical studies, and Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing saw share price increases due to AI chip demand.

feed.title: SMOG ETF Volume Surges 17x as Tesla Slumps
feed.summary: The VanEck Low Carbon Energy ETF (SMOG) experienced an unusual trading volume, surging 17 times its daily average. This surge coincides with Tesla's weaker-than-expected Q1 2026 deliveries, which pressured the stock and led analysts to cut price targets despite long-term AI and robotaxi ambitions. In contrast, Rivian, a smaller holding, reported strong delivery growth and received more optimistic analyst commentary due to its R2 platform and Uber partnership.

Chevron and ConocoPhillips are highlighted as strong performers despite challenges, benefiting from geopolitical tensions pushing fuel and LNG prices higher. Verizon Communications' performance, while not directly related to energy, is currently overshadowed by these commodity-driven market movements.

feed.title: Costco Membership Program: Driving Recurring Revenue and Customer Loyalty in North American Retail
feed.summary: Costco's membership program is a crucial driver of its business success, generating high-margin recurring revenue and fostering strong customer loyalty with renewal rates consistently above 90%. feed.title: 3 Edge AI Stocks to Watch as the Next Wave of AI Demand Builds
feed.summary: The article highlights three companies positioned to benefit from the growing "edge AI" market: Honeywell (HON), Vertiv (VRT), and One Stop Systems (OSS). Edge AI, which processes artificial intelligence directly on devices rather than in the cloud, is expected to see significant demand across various industries. Honeywell is noted for its existing infrastructure in rugged industrial devices, Vertiv for its thermal and power management solutions necessary for distributed edge deployments, and One Stop Systems as a speculative pure-play in defense-grade ruggedized computing.
